name: SambaNova Cloud API
what_it_does: High-speed LLM inference API using SambaNova's custom RDU (Reconfigurable Dataflow Unit) hardware. Offers OpenAI-compatible REST API with exceptionally fast token throughput for Llama, Qwen, and other open models.
use_cases: Running fast LLM inference for latency-sensitive agent workflows requiring high throughput
Accessing open-source models (Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek) via OpenAI-compatible API
Building cost-effective AI pipelines that need to process large volumes of text quickly
Running inference on large context window models (up to 131K tokens) without GPU management
Batch processing document analysis workloads where throughput matters more than latency
not_for: Fine-tuning custom models — SambaNova is inference-only
Organizations needing proprietary frontier models (Claude, GPT-4) not available on SambaNova
best_when: An agent needs the highest possible token throughput for open-source model inference, especially for long context or batch processing workloads.
avoid_when: You need OpenAI's proprietary models or Anthropic Claude — SambaNova only hosts open models.

auth.notes: API key from SambaNova Cloud console. Bearer token in Authorization header. OpenAI-compatible API surface — existing OpenAI SDK clients work with base URL change.

pricing.notes: Pay-per-token pricing competitive with other inference providers. Free credit on signup. Pricing competitive with Groq and Together AI. Known for industry-leading throughput (1000+ tokens/second).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAI-compatible API means existing documentation and client libraries apply. Error responses follow OpenAI format. Model-specific documentation covers context limits and capabilities.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: LLM completions are stateless and inherently idempotent for the same prompt. Temperature=0 produces deterministic outputs.
known_agent_gotchas: OpenAI SDK works but base_url must be changed to SambaNova endpoint
Model names differ from OpenAI — must check SambaNova-specific model IDs
Streaming responses supported but throughput advantage is most visible in non-streaming batch
Rate limits can be hit during peak demand — implement retry with backoff

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 certified. TLS enforced. Standard API key auth. No PII in training data from API calls. Enterprise agreements available for data handling requirements.
